title: Fix Recruitment Workflow
body:
This pull request targets the recruitment workflow seed definitions inside the single-file seeds in base_project/workflow_seed.py. The Recruitment Workflow seed currently establishes a multi-step flow (read input, read resume, read job description, possibly compare, and send follow-ups). The fix focuses on solidifying the step chain and ensuring the completion path is unambiguous, reducing chances of dangling or skipped steps and clarifying the intent of the job-description/resume comparison step.

What this change addresses:
- Ensures the recruitment seed defines a clear start (TRIGGER) and a defined end (COMPLETE) path.
- Aligns step descriptions and transitions to avoid ambiguous or broken flows when the seed is re-run.
- Keeps the existing behavior intact for idempotent seeding, while making the flow more robust against partial seeds or re-seeding in CI/local environments.

Notes:
- No changes to external integrations or business logic beyond the seed wiring, so runtime behavior depends on the underlying models/tools in the repository.
- Tests are not present in this repository snapshot; consider adding targeted tests for AgentWorkflowSeed.build_recruitment_workflow to verify step chaining and completion handling in future iterations.

step_plan.summary: Fix recruitment workflow seed: solidify step chain, ensure explicit end path, and clarify the job-description/resume comparison step; preserve idempotent seeding while improving flow robustness.

steps.operation: Refactor the recruitment workflow seeding to fix step chaining and clarify the resume-vs-job-description comparison logic
steps.reason: The current seed wires Step4 with an ambiguous extra argument and the completion path from Step2 to COMPLETE is potentially brittle. Clarifying the comparison step and ensuring a deterministic COMPLETE path improves robustness during reseed and in CI/local environments.
steps.side_effects: Potential changes to the Recruitment Workflow in the database; may affect idempotent reseeding, downstream automation flows, and test fixtures (if seeded in CI). Requires re-seeding to verify end-to-end path and idempotency; consider adding tests for build_recruitment_workflow in future.
